summary: The gate composes each kind's effective contract from the lock's clause/kind declaration rows, not the temper.toml [kind.*] layer — clause overrides gate from the lock

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: `compose::effective` (compose.rs:969) sources per-kind clause/severity overrides from the lock's `ClauseRow`/`KindFactRow` (drift::Declarations) instead of an `AuthorLayer` `[kind.*]` layer; the layer-over-floor clause path (`layer_over`, compose.rs:874) retires as the contract's clause source. `effective` still yields the two-greens contract — only its override input changes from the temper.toml layer to lock rows. The `AuthorLayer` `[kind.*]` parse survives for the in-place/authority readers TEMPER-TOML-ZERO retires, but no longer feeds any contract.

Feed lock clause/kind rows to `compose::effective` at all three call sites: Schema (main.rs:309/326/332) reads `.temper` declarations for clauses instead of `load_layer`; the gate (main.rs:691) passes clauses off the `declarations` it already reads (main.rs:639). The temper.toml layer stops affecting any contract. `load_layer` itself stays until TEMPER-TOML-ZERO (still feeds in-place).

Migrate any assertion depending on a temper.toml `[kind.*]` layer changing the contract to write the clause/kind override into a golden `.temper/lock.toml` row (as requirements already ride the lock via `write_requirements`). A redundant `[kind.skill] package=` floor re-bind (roster.rs:138) becomes inert (the floor auto-binds by identity) and drops.

The custom-kind registration fixtures (`[kind.spec]`/`[kind.adr]` with clauses, acceptance.rs:215/244/337/352) declared via temper.toml migrate to lock `[[declaration.kind]]`/`[[declaration.clause]]` rows — the six-noun custom-kind path is SDK/lock-authored, never a manifest table.
The temper.toml floor-rebind writes (graph.rs:327) become inert under lock-composition and drop; any assertion resting on a layer-read migrates to a golden lock.
The `[kind.skill]` floor-rebind write (reachable_gate.rs:305) drops as inert; reachability already rides `write_reachability` golden locks.
Migrate any temper.toml `[kind.*]`/layer fixture whose gate effect this change removes to a golden lock row.
Migrate any temper.toml layer fixture the session-start gate depended on to a golden lock; the surface-present branch already reads `.temper`.
Drop inert temper.toml layer writes; the memory gate composes off the floor + lock rows.
The empty-assembly coherence fixtures already read `declarations.clauses`; reconcile any temper.toml layer write to the lock.
Migrate any temper.toml layer fixture the install/gate path depended on to a golden lock row.
Reconcile CLI fixtures that wrote a temper.toml layer to a golden lock; a redundant floor rebind drops.

tests.asserts: a per-kind clause/severity override declared in the lock's `[[declaration.clause]]` rows changes the gate's verdict; the same override written only in a temper.toml `[kind.*]` layer has no effect

acceptance: The gate's effective-contract clauses come from the lock's `ClauseRow`/`KindFactRow`, never a temper.toml `[kind.*]` layer; a lock-declared clause override gates, a temper.toml-only one is inert. cargo test/clippy/fmt green.
notes: The S1–S7 ceremony moved requirements/edges/reachability/authority to the lock but left the effective-contract clause/kind layer on temper.toml (compose::effective, main.rs:691). Emit already writes ClauseRow (Payload.declarations); the gate just never reads it into the contract — a live seam gap. Predecessor to TEMPER-TOML-ZERO: the reader can't retire until clauses gate from the lock. Shares main.rs/compose.rs/tests — serialized ahead of S7b.

summary: Retire every remaining temper.toml reader — load_layer, AuthorLayer::load/authority/fold_local, the in-place [[member]] path, the consts — and sweep all refs to zero

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: Retire `AuthorLayer::load` (compose.rs:664)/`authority` (831)/`fold_local` (925), the in-place read path (`parse_inplace_member` 1416, `inplace_members` 840, `InPlaceMember` 113, the `[[member]]` array parse in `AuthorLayer::parse`), and the `layer_over`/temper.toml plumbing left vestigial after GATE-CONTRACT-FROM-LOCK. The `AuthorLayer::parse` unit tests (compose.rs:1585+) retire with the reader. Sweep the module's temper.toml doc references.

Remove `TEMPER_TOML` (main.rs:50) + `TEMPER_LOCAL_TOML` (56) + every use: gate paths (269/274), Schema (309), explain (430), `load_layer` fn (586), gate (629), the session_start harness-join (614). Retiring `load_layer`/`AuthorLayer::load` cascades to the in-place consumers — `validate_inplace_kinds` (974), `inplace_unit` (1150), the explain `any_inplace` synthesis (469), the inplace arms (1059/1123/1249) — all die with the reader. `authority_from_lock` (549) stays: it already reads the lock. Sweep remaining temper.toml doc comments.
Sweep the temper.toml doc reference (import.rs:6) to the lock vocabulary — the manifest filename is retired.
Rewrite the temper.toml mentions in bundle guidance strings (bundle.rs:109/134) to the lock/SDK vocabulary.
Sweep the temper.toml doc comments (roster.rs:205/595) and retire the `AuthorLayer::parse`-over-`temper.toml` test helper (roster.rs:599) with the reader.
Sweep the temper.toml module-doc reference (schema.rs:5).
Rewrite the user-facing message (read.rs:1311) telling authors to declare `[requirement.<name>]` in temper.toml — requirements ride the lock/SDK program now, never a manifest.
Sweep the temper.toml doc references (contract.rs:6/525) to the lock vocabulary.
Sweep the temper.toml doc references (check.rs:308/573).
Retire the temper.toml test-helper spellings (`AuthorLayer::parse` fragments, graph.rs:1022/1281) with the reader; sweep the doc references (graph.rs:1018/1277).

Zero out every remaining temper.toml write/reference; a file by that name is inert (old temper). In-place `[[member]]` and any residual layer fixtures retire — assembly facts already ride golden locks.
Zero out remaining temper.toml references (post-migration residue).
Zero out remaining temper.toml references.
Zero out remaining temper.toml references.
Zero out remaining temper.toml references.
Zero out remaining temper.toml references.
Zero out remaining temper.toml references.
Zero out remaining temper.toml references.
Zero out remaining temper.toml references.
Assert no verb reads or writes a temper.toml; a file by that name is inert (old temper, never current temper's input). Zero out remaining references.
Zero out the remaining temper.toml reference.

tests.asserts: no verb reads or writes a temper.toml; a file by that name is inert (old temper, never current temper's input)

acceptance: `rg -c 'temper\.toml' src/ tests/` = 0; the manifest filename is retired entirely (20-surface: 'the name temper.toml retires with the manifest era entirely'). cargo test/clippy/fmt green.
notes: Chain terminal. After GATE-CONTRACT-FROM-LOCK, the temper.toml layer feeds no contract, so the reader is vestigial for clauses; this slice pulls the reader outright and the in-place `[[member]]` path S5 kept, then sweeps every doc/message/test reference to zero. Cascade reports (2)-(4) under `(inplace-lock-producer)` close here — no manifest-mode coverage remains. Sole open entry when it runs; ~21 files, serialized behind S7a so overlap is harmless.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.2, PR #4); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.2 07-06); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
